# AVCustomDeviceRoute

**Class**

An object that represents a custom device route.

## Declaration

```swift
class AVCustomDeviceRoute
```

## Overview

Use the value of a route’s [doc://com.apple.avrouting/documentation/AVRouting/AVCustomDeviceRoute/networkEndpoint] or [doc://com.apple.avrouting/documentation/AVRouting/AVCustomDeviceRoute/bluetoothIdentifier] property to establish a connection to a device. Typically, only one of the properties provides a valid value, depending on the type of device. In certain cases, both properties may provide valid values, in which case your app determines which one to use.

## Identifying routes

- **bluetoothIdentifier**: An identifier to use to establish a connection to a Bluetooth device.
- **networkEndpoint**: A local or remote endpoint to connect to.

## Inspecting an event

- **route**: A route for the event.
- **reason**: A reason for an event, such as a user request to activate or deactivate a route.
- **AVCustomRoutingEventReason**: Values that indicate the reason for a routing event.

## Inherits From

- NSObject

## Conforms To

- CVarArg
- CustomDebugStringConvertible
- CustomStringConvertible
- Equatable
- Hashable
- NSObjectProtocol
- Sendable
- SendableMetatype
